requirements.full_sentence:
Some of the NBFCs may also be involved in implementation of various forms of Debt
Relief Schemes (DRS) announced by State Governments that inter alia entail sacrifice
/ waiver of debt obligations of a targeted segment of borrowers, against fiscal support.

requirements.full_sentence:
If such schemes are announced frequently, incommensurately, or without due
consideration to the principles of financial discipline, they would negatively affect credit
discipline and in the long run, may be counter-productive to the credit flow to such
borrowers.
requirements.object: credit flow to such borrowers

requirements.full_sentence:
Part A of these Directions shall apply to a NBFC which is a non-deposit taking
NBFC with asset size of ₹500 crore or more, or a deposit taking NBFC.
requirements.object: which is a non-deposit taking NBFC with asset size of ₹500 crore or more, or a deposit taking NBFC

requirements.full_sentence:
Part B of these Directions shall apply to a NBFC which is a non-deposit taking
NBFC with asset size of less than ₹500 crore.
requirements.object: which is a non-deposit taking NBFC with asset size of less than ₹500 crore

requirements.full_sentence:
NBFCs which are required to comply with Indian Accounting Standards (IndAS)
shall continue to be guided by the Standards and the advisories issued by the
Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI Advisories) in case of any
inconsistencies between these directions and the Standards.
requirements.object: by the Standards and the advisories issued by the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI Advisories) in case of any i

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be called the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking
Financial Companies - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall come into force with immediate effect unless specified
otherwise.
requirements.object: immediate effect unless specified otherwise

requirements.full_sentence:
These Directions shall be applicable to following Non-Banking Finance
Companies (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'NBFCs' and individually as an
'NBFC'), for all layers unless specified otherwise:
(1) NBFC-D registered with the RBI under the provisions of the RBI Act,
1934;
requirements.object: Non-Banking Finance Companies (hereinafter collectively referred to as 'NBFCs' and individually as an 'NBFC'), for all laye

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions given in paragraphs 86 to 100 shall apply to MGC registered with
RBI under the scheme of Registration of Mortgage Guarantee Companies.
requirements.object: with RBI under the scheme of Registration of Mortgage Guarantee Companies

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions given in paragraphs 86 to 101 and paragraphs 102 to 138 shall
apply to HFC registered with RBI under the provisions of the NHB Act, 1987.
requirements.object: with RBI under the provisions of the NHB Act, 1987

requirements.full_sentence:
In Part A of these Directions, the following definitions shall apply, unless the
context otherwise requires:
(1) 'aggregate exposure' shall include all fund based and non-fund based
exposure, including investment exposure;

(2) 'compromise settlement' shall refer to any negotiated arrangement with
the borrower to fully settle the claims of a NBFC against the borrower in
cash.
requirements.object: arrangement with the borrower to fully settle the claims of a NBFC against the borrower in cash

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: Compromise settlement may entail some sacrifice of the
amount due from the borrower on the part of the NBFC with
corresponding waiver of claims of the NBFC against the borrower to that
extent.
requirements.object: the amount due from the borrower on the part of the NBFC with corresponding waiver of claims of the NBFC against the borrower

(3) 'credit event' in the context of projects under implementation shall be
deemed to have been triggered on the occurrence of any of the following:
Explanation: Only equity and instruments compulsorily convertible into
equity (without any embedded optionality) shall be exempt from
determining outstanding principal debt.

principal debt envisaged to be held by all the specified lenders as per the proposed resolution plan

requirements.full_sentence:
(11) 'resolution plan' in the context of projects under implementation shall
mean a mutually agreed, legally binding, feasible and time-bound plan
for resolution of stress in a project finance account.
requirements.object: legally binding, feasible and time-bound plan for resolution of stress in a project finance account

requirements.full_sentence:
The resolution plan
may involve any action / plan / reorganization including, but not limited
to, regularisation of the account by payment of all overdues by the debtor
entity, sale of the exposures to other entities / investors, change in
ownership, extension of DCCO and restructuring.
requirements.object: plan / reorganization including, but not limited to, regularisation of the account by payment of all overdues by the debtor entit

(12) 'restructuring' shall mean an act in which a NBFC, for economic or legal
reasons relating to the borrower's financial difficulty, grants concessions
to the borrower.
requirements.object: which a NBFC, for economic or legal reasons relating to the borrower's financial difficulty, grants concessions to the borrower

requirements.full_sentence:
The terms 'Appointed Date, Commercial Real Estate (CRE)', 'Commercial Real
Estate - Housing (CRE-RH)', 'Construction phase', 'Date of Financial Closure',
'Infrastructure Sector', 'Original Date of Commencement of Commercial
Operations (Original DCCO)', 'Extended DCCO', 'Actual DCCO', 'Project', and
'Project Finance' shall have the same meaning assigned to them in the Reserve
Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Credit Facilities) Directions,
2025.
requirements.object: assigned to them in the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
All other expressions, unless defined herein, shall have the same meaning as
have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the
Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or the Companies Act, 2013, or any statutory
modification or re-enactment thereto or other regulations issued by the Reserve
Bank of India or the Glossary of Terms published by Reserve Bank or as used in
commercial parlance, as the case may be.
requirements.object: as have been assigned to them under the Banking Regulation Act, 1949 or the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 or the Companies Act

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC shall put in place Board-approved policies for resolution of stressed
assets, including the timelines for resolution as well as detailed policies on
various signs of financial difficulty, providing quantitative as well as qualitative
parameters, for determining financial difficulty.
requirements.object: policies for resolution of stressed assets, including the timelines for resolution as well as detailed policies on various

requirements.full_sentence:
The board approved policy of the NBFC shall incorporate provisions for
resolution as provided for under Chapter VI-A of these Directions, including the
following:
(1) the objective principles for the terms of relief to be granted to various
borrower / loan categories.
requirements.object: as provided for under Chapter VI-A of these Directions, including the following: (1) the objective principles fo

requirements.full_sentence:
A non-exhaustive indicative list of signs of financial difficulty, which is based on
the Basel Committee Guidelines on 'Prudential treatment of problem assets -
definitions of non-performing exposures and forbearance', is provided as under
for reference:
(1) A default, as per the definition provided in the framework, shall be treated
as an indicator for financial difficulty, irrespective of reasons for the
default.
requirements.object: indicator for financial difficulty, irrespective of reasons for the default

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC shall complement the above list of non-exhaustive enumeration of
financial difficulty indicators with key financial ratios and operational parameters
which may include quantitative and qualitative aspects.
requirements.object: of non-exhaustive enumeration of financial difficulty indicators with key financial ratios and operational parameters which

on an exposure.
requirements.object: the absence of arrears on an exposure

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC shall put in place Board-approved policies for undertaking compromise
settlements with the borrowers as well as for technical write-offs, which shall inter
alia include the following:
(1) comprehensive prescription of the process to be followed for all
compromise settlements and technical write-offs, with specific guidance
on the necessary conditions precedent such as minimum ageing,
deterioration in collateral value etc.;
requirements.object: policies for undertaking compromise settlements with the borrowers as well as for technical write-offs, which shall inter
Board

(i) delegation of power for such approvals rests with an authority
(individual or committee, as the case may be) which is at least one
level higher in hierarchy than the authority vested with power to
sanction the credit / investment exposure.
requirements.object: least one level higher in hierarchy than the authority vested with power to sanction the credit / investment exposure

Provided that any official who was part of sanctioning the loan (as

classified as fraud or wilful defaulter, as permitted in terms of

requirements.object: Board in all cases

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC shall recognise incipient stress in loan accounts, immediately on default,
by classifying such assets as special mention accounts (SMA) as per the
following categories:
Basis for classification - Principal or interest payment or any other amount wholly or partly overdue
SMA Sub- categories
SMA-0 Up to 30 days
SMA-1 More than 30 days and up to 60 days
SMA-2 More than 60 days and up to 90 days
requirements.object: loan accounts, immediately on default, by classifying such assets as special mention accounts (SMA) as per the following

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC shall report credit information, including classification of an account as
SMA, to Central Repository of Information on Large Credits (CRILC), on all
borrowers having aggregate exposure of ₹5 crore and above with them, on a
monthly basis.
requirements.object: classification of an account as SMA, to Central Repository of Information on Large Credits (CRILC), on all borrow

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC shall submit a weekly report of instances of default by all borrowers (with
aggregate exposure of ₹5 crore and above) by close of business on every Friday,
or the preceding working day if Friday happens to be a holiday.
requirements.object: of instances of default by all borrowers (with aggregate exposure of ₹5 crore and above) by close of business on every Friday,

requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where a NBFC fails to report SMA status of the accounts to CRILC or
resort to methods with the intent to conceal the actual status of the accounts or
evergreen the account, the NBFCs shall be subjected to accelerated provisioning
for these accounts and/ or other supervisory actions as deemed appropriate by
the Reserve Bank.
requirements.object: provisioning for these accounts and/ or other supervisory actions as deemed appropriate by the Reserve Bank

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC shall adhere to the relevant provisions on submission of financial
information to information utilities of Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 and
Insolvency and Bankruptcy Board of India (Information Utilities) Regulations,
2017 and put in place appropriate systems and procedures to ensure compliance
to the provisions of the Code and Regulations.
requirements.object: provisions on submission of financial information to information utilities of Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 and Insolven

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC shall make suitable disclosures in its financial statements in the 'Notes
to Accounts', as specified in the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial
Companies - Financial Statements: Presentation and Disclosures) Directions,
2025.
requirements.object: its financial statements in the 'Notes to Accounts', as specified in the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Com

requirements.full_sentence:
Once a borrower is reported to be in default by any of the specified lenders other
than a NBFC, the specified lenders shall jointly undertake a prima facie review of
the borrower account within the Review Period.
requirements.object: facie review of the borrower account within the Review Period

requirements.full_sentence:
During this Review Period, the specified lenders shall jointly decide on the
resolution strategy, including the nature of the resolution plan, the approach for
implementation of the resolution plan, etc.
requirements.object: resolution strategy, including the nature of the resolution plan, the approach for implementation of the resolution plan, etc

requirements.full_sentence: A NBFC may also choose to initiate legal proceedings for insolvency or recovery.
requirements.object: legal proceedings for insolvency or recovery

conditions: where resolution plan is to be implemented
requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where resolution plan is to be implemented, all specified lenders, shall
enter into an inter-creditor agreement, during the Review Period, to provide for
ground rules for finalisation and implementation of the resolution plan in respect
of borrowers with credit facilities from more than one specified lender.
requirements.object: agreement, during the Review Period, to provide for ground rules for finalisation and implementation of the resolution pl

requirements.full_sentence:
The inter-creditor agreement shall provide that:
(1) any decision agreed by signatories representing 75 per cent by value of
total outstanding credit facilities (fund based as well non-fund based) and
60 per cent of signatories by number shall be binding upon all the
signatories;
requirements.object: decision agreed by signatories representing 75 per cent by value of total outstanding credit facilities (fund based as well non-

requirements.full_sentence:
and
(2) resolution plans shall provide for payment not less than the liquidation
value due to the dissenting signatories.
requirements.object: less than the liquidation value due to the dissenting signatories

requirements.full_sentence:
In addition to the requirements in paragraph 32 above, the inter-creditor
agreement shall, inter alia, provide for rights and duties of majority signatories,
duties and protection of rights of dissenting signatories, treatment of signatories
with priority in cash flows / differential security interest, etc.
requirements.object: for rights and duties of majority signatories, duties and protection of rights of dissenting signatories, treatment of signatori

A resolution plan may involve any action / plan / reorganization including, but not
limited to, regularisation of the account by payment of all over dues by the
borrower entity, sale of the exposures to other entities / investors, change in
ownership and restructuring.

where the aggregate exposure of specified lenders is ₹100 crore and

A resolution plan involving restructuring / change in ownership in respect of
accounts where the aggregate exposure of specified lenders is ₹100 crore and
above, shall require independent credit evaluation of the residual debt by credit
rating agencies specifically authorised by the Reserve Bank for this purpose.
requirements.object: of the residual debt by credit rating agencies specifically authorised by the Reserve Bank for this purpose

While accounts with aggregate exposure of ₹500 crore and above shall require
two such independent credit evaluations, others shall require one independent
credit evaluation.
requirements.object: credit evaluations, others shall require one independent credit evaluation

requirements.full_sentence:
Only such resolution plans which receive a credit opinion of RP4 or better for the
residual debt from one or two credit rating agencies, as the case may be, shall
be considered for implementation.

conditions:
If independent credit evaluation is obtained from more than the required number
of credit rating agencies
requirements.full_sentence:
If independent credit evaluation is obtained from more than the required number
of credit rating agencies, all such independent credit evaluation opinions shall be
RP4 or better for the resolution plan to be considered for implementation.
requirements.object: for the resolution plan to be considered for implementation

requirements.full_sentence:
The credit rating agencies shall be directly engaged by the specified lenders for
the purpose of independent credit evaluation and the payment of fee for such
assignments shall be made by the specified lenders.
requirements.object: the specified lenders for the purpose of independent credit evaluation and the payment of fee for such assignments shall be mad

The process of re-
classification of an asset should not stop merely because RP is under

continue to have credit exposure, shall be deemed to be 'implemented' only if the
following conditions are met:
(1) A resolution plan which does not involve restructuring / change in
ownership shall be deemed to be implemented only if the borrower is not
in default with any of the specified lenders as on 180th day from the end

assigning the exposures to third party or a resolution plan involving recovery

fully extinguished.

implemented within 180 days from the end of Review Period, which shall
commence not later than:
(1) the reference date, if in default as on the reference date;
requirements.object: days from the end of Review Period, which shall commence not later than: (1) the reference date

Less than ₹1500 crore To be announced in due course

The additional specific provisions shall also be required to be made in cases

The additional specific provisions specified in paragraph 48 may be reversed as
under:
(1) where the resolution plan involves only payment of overdues by the
borrower - the additional provisions may be reversed only if the borrower
is not in default for a period of six months from the date of clearing of the

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of this Chapter shall be applicable to all restructurings, including
those undertaken under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016.
requirements.object: restructurings, including those undertaken under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016

requirements.full_sentence:
An account classified as 'standard' shall be immediately downgraded as non-
performing assets, i.e., 'sub-standard' to begin with, following a restructuring by
a NBFC.
requirements.object: non- performing assets, i

requirements.full_sentence:
An account classified as non-performing asset, upon restructuring by a NBFC,
shall continue to have the same asset classification as prior to restructuring.
requirements.object: same asset classification as prior to restructuring

requirements.full_sentence:
In both above cases, the asset classification shall continue to be governed by
the ageing criteria as per extant asset classification norms contained in the
Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Income
Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: by the ageing criteria as per extant asset classification norms contained in the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial

requirements.full_sentence:
Any additional finance approved under the resolution plan (including any
resolution plan approved by the Adjudicating Authority under Insolvency and
Bankruptcy Code, 2016) may be treated by a NBFC as standard asset during the
monitoring period under the approved resolution plan, provided the account
demonstrates satisfactory performance during the monitoring period.
requirements.object: NBFC as standard asset during the monitoring period under the approved resolution plan

requirements.full_sentence:
If the restructured asset fails to perform satisfactorily during the monitoring period
or does not qualify for upgradation at the end of the monitoring period, the
additional finance shall be placed in the same asset classification category as the
restructured debt by a NBFC.
requirements.object: same asset classification category as the restructured debt by a NBFC

Code, 2016] extended by the specified lenders to borrowers undergoing

requirements.object: asset during the insolvency resolution process period as defined in the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016

requirements.full_sentence:

retained in the same category on restructuring by a NBFC may be upgraded only

monitoring period.

requirements.full_sentence:

commencement of the first payment of interest or principal (whichever is later) on
the credit facility with longest period of moratorium under the terms of RP.

conditions:
where the aggregate
exposure of specified lenders is ₹100 crore and above at the time of

exposure of specified lenders is ₹100 crore and above at the time of
implementation of resolution plan, to qualify for an upgrade, in addition to
demonstration of satisfactory performance, the credit facilities of the borrower

loan ratings.

if the ratings are obtained from more than the required
number of credit rating agencies

requirements.object: the account to qualify for an upgrade

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: These ratings referred to in the second proviso shall be the normal
ratings provided by the credit rating agencies and not Independent Credit
Evaluation specified in paragraph 36.
requirements.object: provided by the credit rating agencies and not Independent Credit Evaluation specified in paragraph 36

requirements.full_sentence:

requirements.object: of a fresh restructuring / change in ownership under these Directions or under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016

requirements.full_sentence:

Financial Companies - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and
Provisioning) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: the asset classification category as laid out in the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Income Recog

requirements.full_sentence:

the resolution plan by the Adjudicating Authority in terms of section 31 (1) of the

requirements.full_sentence:
Subsequent to the lapse of the period specified in paragraph 70, provisioning
shall be as per the requirements in the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking
Financial Companies - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and
Provisioning) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: requirements in the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning)

conditions:
if the Adjudicating Authority rejects the resolution plan thus
submitted
requirements.full_sentence:
The facility of freezing of provisions as allowed in paragraph 70 shall lapse
immediately if the Adjudicating Authority rejects the resolution plan thus
submitted, and asset classification in respect of such borrower shall continue be
governed by the extant asset classification norms.
requirements.object: Adjudicating Authority rejects the resolution plan thus submitted, and asset classification in respect of such borrower shall

requirements.full_sentence:
Interest income in respect of restructured accounts classified as 'standard assets'
may be recognized on accrual basis and that in respect of the restructured
accounts classified as 'non-performing assets' shall be recognised on cash basis.
requirements.object: basis

conditions:
where the pre-restructuring facilities
were classified as non-performing assets
requirements.full_sentence:
In the case of additional finance in accounts where the pre-restructuring facilities
were classified as non-performing assets, the interest income shall be recognised
<image>
only on cash basis except when the restructuring is accompanied by a change in
ownership.
requirements.object: on cash basis except when the restructuring is accompanied by a change in ownership

conditions: In case of change in ownership of the borrowing entity
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of change in ownership of the borrowing entity, credit facilities of the
concerned borrowing entities may be continued / upgraded as 'standard' by a
NBFC after the change in ownership is implemented, either under the Insolvency
and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 or under these Directions.
requirements.object: as 'standard' by a NBFC after the change in ownership is implemented, either under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016 or

conditions: If the change in ownership is implemented under these Directions
requirements.full_sentence:
If the change in ownership is implemented under these Directions, then the
classification as 'standard' shall be subject to the following conditions:
(1) The NBFC shall conduct necessary due diligence in this regard and
clearly establish that the acquirer is not a person disqualified in terms of
Section 29A of the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code, 2016.
requirements.object: following conditions: (1) The NBFC shall conduct necessary due diligence in this regard and clearly establish that the acquirer is n

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The NBFC shall clearly establish that the 'new promoter' is not a person
/ entity / subsidiary / associate etc.
requirements.object: 'new promoter' is not a person / entity / subsidiary / associate etc

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The new promoter shall have acquired at least 26 per cent of the paid-up
equity capital as well as voting rights of the borrower entity and shall be
the single largest shareholder of the borrower entity.
requirements.object: 26 per cent of the paid-up equity capital as well as voting rights of the borrower entity and shall be the single largest share

requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The new promoter shall be in 'control' of the borrower entity as per the
definition of 'control' in the Companies Act, 2013 / regulations issued by
the Securities and Exchange Board of India / any other applicable
regulations / accounting standards, as the case may be.
requirements.object: the borrower entity as per the definition of 'control' in the Companies Act, 2013 / regulations issued by the Securities and Exchan

conditions:
If the account fails to perform satisfactorily at any point of time during the
monitoring period
requirements.full_sentence:
If the account fails to perform satisfactorily at any point of time during the
monitoring period, it shall trigger a fresh Review Period.
requirements.object: Period

requirements.full_sentence:
The quantum of provisions held (excluding additional provisions) by the NBFC
against the said account as on the date of change in ownership of the borrowing
entities may be reversed only after the end of monitoring period subject to
satisfactory performance during the same.
requirements.object: the end of monitoring period subject to satisfactory performance during the same

requirements.full_sentence:
In case the promoters' shares have been pledged with a NBFC as collateral
during the lock-in period mandated under Securities and Exchange Board of India
(Issue of Capital and Disclosure Requirements) (ICDR) Regulations, 2018, and
the pledge is invoked during the lock-in period by the NBFC consequent to default
by the company, such shares may be transferred to the NBFC but shall continue
to be under lock-in in the hands of the NBFC for the remaining lock-in period.
requirements.object: lock-in in the hands of the NBFC for the remaining lock-in period

conditions:
if all the following
conditions are met:
(1) the seller of the assets is in financial difficulty
requirements.full_sentence:
A sale and leaseback transaction of the assets of a borrower or other transactions
of similar nature shall be treated as an event of restructuring for the purpose of
asset classification and provisioning in the books of a NBFC with regard to the
residual debt of the seller as well as the debt of the buyer if all the following
conditions are met:
(1) the seller of the assets is in financial difficulty;
requirements.object: event of restructuring for the purpose of asset classification and provisioning in the books of a NBFC with regard to the residual de

requirements.full_sentence:
Borrowers who have committed frauds/ malfeasance/ wilful default as well as any
entity with which a wilful defaulter is associated shall remain ineligible for
restructuring.

However, in cases where the existing promoters are replaced by new promoters
satisfying the conditions specified at sub-paragraphs 77(1) to 77(4)_bookmark20,
and the borrower company is totally delinked from such erstwhile promoters /
management, a NBFC may take a view on restructuring such accounts based on
their viability, without prejudice to the continuance of criminal action against the
erstwhile promoters / management.
requirements.object: restructuring such accounts based on their viability, without prejudice to the continuance of criminal action against the erstwhile pro

requirements.full_sentence:
A wilful defaulter or any entity with which a wilful defaulter is associated shall be
eligible for restructuring subsequent to removal of the name of wilful defaulter
from the List of Wilful Defaulters, subject to penal measures applicable to
borrowers classified as wilful defaulter in terms of the Reserve Bank of India
(Non-Banking Financial Companies - Treatment of Wilful Defaulters and Large
Defaulters) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: subsequent to removal of the name of wilful defaulter from the List of Wilful Defaulters

requirements.full_sentence:
The objective of compromise settlements shall be to maximise the possible
recovery from a distressed borrower at minimum expense, in the best interest of
the NBFC.
requirements.object: possible recovery from a distressed borrower at minimum expense, in the best interest of the NBFC

requirements.full_sentence:
The compromise settlements and technical write-offs shall be without prejudice
to any mutually agreed contractual provisions between a NBFC and a borrower
relating to future contingent realizations or recovery by the NBFC, subject to such
claims not being recognised in any manner on the balance sheet of the NBFC at
the time of the settlement or subsequently till actual realization of such
receivables.
requirements.object: any mutually agreed contractual provisions between a NBFC and a borrower relating to future contingent realizations or recovery by the NBFC

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that any such claims recognised on the balance sheet of the NBFC shall
render the arrangement to be treated as restructuring.
requirements.object: be treated as restructuring

requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding paragraph 88, compromise settlements where the time for
payment of the agreed settlement amount exceeds three months shall be treated
as restructuring.

Any arrangement involving part settlement with the borrower shall also fall under
the definition of restructuring and shall be governed by the provisions applicable
thereto.
requirements.object: definition of restructuring and shall be governed by the provisions applicable thereto

requirements.full_sentence:
However, such technical write-offs do not entail any waiver of claims against the
borrower and thus the NBFC's right to recovery shall not undermined in any
manner.

conditions: In case of partial technical write-offs
requirements.full_sentence:
In case of partial technical write-offs, the prudential requirements in respect of
residual exposure, including provisioning and asset classification, shall be with
reference to the original exposure.
requirements.object: the original exposure

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
Provided that the amount of provision including the amount representing partial
technical write-off shall meet the extant provisioning requirements, as computed
on the gross value of the asset.
requirements.object: requirements, as computed on the gross value of the asset

requirements.full_sentence:
There shall be a reporting mechanism to the next higher authority, at least on a
quarterly basis, with respect to compromise settlements and technical write offs
approved by a particular authority.
requirements.object: to the next higher authority, at least on a quarterly basis, with respect to compromise settlements and technical write offs

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that compromise settlements and technical write-offs approved by the
MD & CEO / Board Level Committee shall be reported to the Board.
requirements.object: Board

requirements.full_sentence:
The Board shall mandate a suitable reporting format so as to ensure adequate
coverage of the following aspects at the minimum:
(1) trend in number of accounts and amounts subjected to compromise
settlement and/or technical write-off (q-o-q and y-o-y);
requirements.object: format so as to ensure adequate coverage of the following aspects at the minimum: (1) trend in number of accounts and amo

requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of borrowers subject to compromise settlements, there shall be a
cooling period as determined by the respective Board approved policies before
the NBFC can assume fresh exposures to such borrowers.
requirements.object: as determined by the respective Board approved policies before the NBFC can assume fresh exposures to such borrowers

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that the cooling period in respect of exposures shall be subject to a floor
of 12 months with a NBFC being free to stipulate higher cooling periods in terms
of their Board approved policies.
requirements.object: floor of 12 months with a NBFC being free to stipulate higher cooling periods in terms of their Board approved policies

requirements.full_sentence:
The cooling period to be adopted in respect of exposures subjected to technical
write-offs shall be as per the Board approved policies of a NBFC.
requirements.object: Board approved policies of a NBFC

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC may undertake compromise settlements or technical write-offs in respect
of accounts categorised as wilful defaulters or fraud without prejudice to the
criminal proceeding underway against such borrowers.
requirements.object: technical write-offs in respect of accounts categorised as wilful defaulters or fraud without prejudice to the cri

conditions:
where a NBFC enter
into compromise settlement with such borrowers
requirements.full_sentence:
The penal measures applicable to borrowers classified as fraud or wilful defaulter
in terms of the Reserve Bank of India (Fraud Risk Management in NBFCs)
Directions, 2024 and the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial
Companies - Treatment of Wilful Defaulters and Large Defaulters) Directions,
2025, respectively, shall continue to be applicable in cases where a NBFC enter
into compromise settlement with such borrowers, and the cooling periods
specified in paragraphs 95 and 96, in respect of such borrowers, shall be without
prejudice to such penal measures.
requirements.object: in cases where a NBFC enter into compromise settlement with such borrowers, and the cooling periods specified in paragraphs

requirements.full_sentence:
As long as larger policy concerns are
suitably addressed and the costs of malafide actions are made to be borne by
the perpetrators, early recoveries by a NBFC should be a preferred option,
subject to safeguards.

The compromise settlements with the borrowers under these Directions shall be
without prejudice to the provisions of any other statute in force.
requirements.object: the provisions of any other statute in force

requirements.full_sentence:
Wherever a NBFC had commenced recovery proceedings under a judicial forum
and the same is pending before such judicial forum, any settlement arrived at with
the borrower shall be subject to obtaining a consent decree from the concerned
judicial authorities.
requirements.object: a consent decree from the concerned judicial authorities

conditions: where financial closure has been achieved as on October 1
requirements.full_sentence:
The instructions contained in paragraphs 104 to 122 shall not apply to projects
where financial closure has been achieved as on October 1, 2025, for which the
prudential guidelines on project finance prevailing before October 1, 2025, which
otherwise shall be treated as repealed, shall apply.
requirements.object: financial closure has been achieved as on October 1, 2025, for which the prudential guidelines on project finance prevailing b

requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the instructions in paragraph 102 , any resolution of a fresh credit
event and/or change in material terms and conditions in the loan contract in such
projects, on or after October 1, 2025, shall be as per the guidelines contained in
paragraphs 104 to 122.
requirements.object: guidelines contained in paragraphs 104 to 122

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC shall monitor the performance of the project and any buildup of stress
on an ongoing basis and shall be expected to initiate a resolution plan well in
advance.
requirements.object: the project and any buildup of stress on an ongoing basis and shall be expected to initiate a resolution plan well in advan

requirements.full_sentence:
Occurrence of a credit event with any lender during the construction phase, shall
trigger a collective resolution in terms of Chapter III of these Directions.
requirements.object: in terms of Chapter III of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
Explanation: The reference to 'default' in Chapter III of these Directions shall be
read as 'credit event' for the purpose of project finance accounts, unless specified
otherwise.
requirements.object: event' for the purpose of project finance accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
Any such credit event with a NBFC shall be reported to the Central Repository of
Information on Large Credit (CRILC) by the NBFC in the prescribed weekly as
well as the CRILC-Main report in compliance with paragraphs 20 and 21.
requirements.object: Central Repository of Information on Large Credit (CRILC) by the NBFC in the prescribed weekly as well as the CRILC-Main report in

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC, which is part of a consortium / multiple lending arrangement shall also
report occurrence of such credit event to all other members of the consortium /
multiple lending arrangement.
requirements.object: such credit event to all other members of the consortium / multiple lending arrangement

requirements.full_sentence:
The instructions on CRILC reporting as per paragraph 107 shall be issued in due
course.
requirements.object: course

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC shall undertake a prima facie review of the borrower account during the
Review Period.
requirements.object: review of the borrower account during the Review Period

requirements.full_sentence:
The conduct of a NBFC during the Review Period, including signing of Inter
Creditor Agreement (ICA), and the decision to implement a resolution plan,
wherever required, shall be guided by Chapter III of these Directions, unless
specified otherwise.
requirements.object: III of these Directions

conditions: If a resolution plan involving extension of original / extended DCCO
requirements.full_sentence:
If a resolution plan involving extension of original / extended DCCO, as the case
maybe, is implemented in a project finance account, which is classified as
Standard and satisfies all relevant prudential conditions specified for sanction,
disbursement and monitoring of project finance in the Reserve Bank of India
(Non-Banking Financial Companies - Credit Facilities) Directions, 2025, the
asset classification of such account shall continue to be classified as 'Standard',
provided the envisaged resolution plan ab initio conforms to the following
conditions:
(1) Permitted DCCO Deferment - Original / extended DCCO, as the case
may be, is extended, along with the consequential shift in repayment
requirements.object: as 'Standard'

conditions:
where aggregate exposure of all lenders
is less than ₹100 crore
requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Cost Overrun - A NBFC may finance, as part of a resolution plan, cost
overrun associated with permitted DCCO deferment in compliance with
sub-paragraph (1) above, and classify the account as 'Standard', as
under:
Explanation: For projects where aggregate exposure of all lenders
is less than ₹100 crore, internal credit rating may be considered, if
the project was originally not credit rated externally.
requirements.object: a resolution plan, cost overrun associated with permitted DCCO deferment in compliance with sub-paragraph (1) above, and classify the account as 'Standard', as under: Explanation: For projects where aggregate exposure of all lenders is less than ₹100 crore, internal credit rating may be considered

requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Change in Scope and Size - A project finance account where DCCO
extension is necessitated by an increase in the project outlay on account
of increase in scope and size of the project, may be classified as
'Standard', subject to complying with the following conditions:

The standard asset classification benefit on account of 'change in scope' shall be
allowed only once during the lifetime of the project.
requirements.object: during the lifetime of the project

requirements.full_sentence:
In all the cases specified in paragraph 111, the following conditions shall be
required to be met in respect of all the lenders before the expiry of 180 days from
the end of the Review Period, for successful implementation of a resolution plan:
(1) all required documentation, including execution of necessary agreements
between a lender and the borrower / creation of security charge /
perfection of securities, are completed in consonance with the resolution
plan being implemented;
requirements.object: met in respect of all the lenders before the expiry of 180 days from the end of the Review Period, for successful implementation of

conditions:
If a resolution plan involving change in DCCO is not successfully implemented in
terms of paragraph 111 and / or 113
requirements.full_sentence:
If a resolution plan involving change in DCCO is not successfully implemented in
terms of paragraph 111 and / or 113, then the account shall be downgraded to
NPA immediately.
requirements.object: immediately

requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: Satisfactory performance as defined in sub-paragraph 10(14) shall
apply in this case.

A project finance account downgraded to NPA for non-compliance with
paragraph 113, may be upgraded on successful implementation of resolution
plan, provided no further request for deferment of DCCO is received.
requirements.object: implementation of resolution plan

requirements.full_sentence:
Income recognition in respect of a project finance account where a resolution
plans involving Extension of Original / Extended DCCO shall be as follows:
(1) A NBFC may recognise income on accrual basis in respect of project
finance exposures which are classified as 'Standard'.
requirements.object: A NBFC may recognise income on accrual basis in respect of project finance exposures which are classified as 'Standard'

requirements.full_sentence:
(2) For NPAs, income recognition shall be as per instructions contained in
the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Income
Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: contained in the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisio

requirements.full_sentence:
For accounts which have availed DCCO deferment as per paragraphs 111 to 114
and are classified as 'standard', a NBFC shall maintain additional specific
provisions of 0.375% for infrastructure project loans and 0.5625% for non-
infrastructure project loans (including CRE and CRE-RH), for each quarter of
deferment, over and above the applicable standard asset provision specified in
the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Income
Recognition, Asset Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: of 0

requirements.full_sentence:
The additional specific provisions required as per paragraph 118 shall be
reversed upon commencement of commercial operation.
requirements.object: of commercial operation

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions stipulated in paragraph 118 shall not be applicable for existing
projects which are specified in paragraph 102 and such project loans shall
continue to be guided by the prudential guidelines for the purpose of provisioning,
prevailing before October 1, 2025, shall apply, which otherwise shall be treated
as repealed.
requirements.object: projects which are specified in paragraph 102 and such project loans shall continue to be guided by the prudential guidelin

conditions: if these were sanctioned on or after October 1
requirements.full_sentence:
Notwithstanding the instructions in paragraph 120, in case of any resolution of a
fresh credit event and / or change in material terms and conditions in the loan
contract in such projects, the provisions stipulated in paragraph 118 shall apply
to these projects as if these were sanctioned on or after October 1, 2025.
requirements.object: as if these were sanctioned on or after October 1, 2025

requirements.full_sentence:
Provisioning for project loans classified as 'standard' (construction or operational
phase) or NPA shall be as per extant instructions contained in the Reserve Bank
of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Income Recognition, Asset
Classification and Provisioning) Directions, 2025.
requirements.object: instructions contained in the Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Income Recognition, Asset Classification and P

requirements.full_sentence:
The instructions contained in this Chapter shall be applicable to resolution of
exposures of borrowers impacted by a natural calamity or, mutatis mutandis,
exposures of borrowers impacted by external events such as riots /
disturbances that result in loss to economic activity (hereinafter collectively
referred to as 'calamity'), upon the declaration of such calamity by the Central /
State Governments (in accordance with the framework placed by the
concerned Government for this purpose).
requirements.object: of exposures of borrowers impacted by a natural calamity or, mutatis mutandis, exposures of borrowers impacted by external

conditions:
where relief measures
have already been provided as on the effective date of these Amendment
Directions
requirements.full_sentence:
These instructions shall not apply to borrower accounts where relief measures
have already been provided as on the effective date of these Amendment
Directions, and such accounts shall continue to be guided by the existing
prudential guidelines.
requirements.object: where relief measures have already been provided as on the effective date of these Amendment Directions, and such accounts

requirements.full_sentence:
However, any fresh resolution in such accounts
subsequent to the effective date of these Amendment Directions shall be as
per the guidelines contained in this Chapter.
requirements.object: guidelines contained in this Chapter

requirements.full_sentence:
The decisions taken in the special State Level Bankers' Committee (SLBC) /
Union Territory Level Bankers' Committee (UTLBC) / District Consultative
Committee (DCC) meeting shall be communicated to the NBFCs by the
convenor of the respective SLBC/ DCC.
requirements.object: NBFCs by the convenor of the respective SLBC/ DCC

requirements.full_sentence:
The decisions taken in the special SLBC / UTLBC / DCC meeting(s) shall be
given adequate publicity by NBFCs through various methods such as
brochures, banners, advertisement in newspapers, visits by field staff, and
other suitable modes, for the benefit of affected borrowers.
requirements.object: by NBFCs through various methods such as brochures, banners, advertisement in newspapers, visits by field staff, and other

conditions: Where the SLBC / UTLBC / DCC
requirements.full_sentence:
Where the SLBC / UTLBC / DCC, as the case may be, recommends extending
relief measures to the impacted borrowers, NBFC may implement resolution
plans in respect of such borrowers in terms of the provisions of this Chapter.
requirements.object: measures to the impacted borrowers, NBFC may implement resolution plans in respect of such borrowers in terms of the p

conditions:
In case no specific date of occurrence is ascertainable in respect
of a calamity
requirements.full_sentence:
Explanation: In case no specific date of occurrence is ascertainable in respect
of a calamity, the date of declaration of such calamity by the Central / State
Governments shall be treated as the date of occurrence.
requirements.object: date of occurrence

requirements.full_sentence:
Borrowers, whose loan accounts do not fulfil the required eligibility conditions
for resolution under this Chapter may continue to be considered for resolution
under other provisions of these Directions.
requirements.object: for resolution under other provisions of these Directions

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of this Chapter shall not apply to the refinance portfolio of a
NBFC.
requirements.object: portfolio of a NBFC

requirements.full_sentence:
Those borrowers shall be eligible for resolution under this Chapter whose
accounts are classified as 'Standard', but which are not in default for more than
7 Inserted with effect from July 1, 2026, vide Reserve Bank of India (Non-Banking Financial Companies - Resolution of Stressed Assets) Amendment Directions, 2026 dated April 29, 2026.
requirements.object: under this Chapter whose accounts are classified as 'Standard', but which are not in default for more than 7 Inserted with

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC may decide on participating in a particular DRS notified by a
Government, based on its Board approved policy, subject to the extant regulatory
norms.
requirements.object: a particular DRS notified by a Government, based on its Board approved policy

requirements.full_sentence:
Any provision of the scheme that may warrant modification in long term interest
of the borrowers or for prudential reasons may be duly brought to the notice of
the concerned authority/ies through the State Level Bankers' Committee / District
level Consultative Committee, during the consultation phase while designing the
DRS.
requirements.object: term interest of the borrowers or for prudential reasons may be duly brought to the notice of the concerned authority/ies

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC shall clearly determine the eventual outstanding that may crystallise in
their books in respect of the borrowers proposed to be covered under the DRS,
including the accumulated interest in non-performing accounts, by the time the
dues are settled under the DRS, to enable the Government to suitably arrange
for the extent of fiscal participation.
requirements.object: outstanding that may crystallise in their books in respect of the borrowers proposed to be covered under the DRS, inclu

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC shall ensure that the borrowers to be covered under DRS are selected
strictly as per terms of such schemes so as to avoid subsequent non-admission
by the authorities on technical grounds.
requirements.object: to be covered under DRS are selected strictly as per terms of such schemes so as to avoid subsequent non-admission by the au

requirements.full_sentence:
The terms and conditions of the scheme as well as the prudential aspects,
including cooling period for extending fresh credit, impact on credit score etc.,
shall be clearly communicated to the borrowers at the time of obtaining explicit
consent from the borrower for availing benefits under a proposed DRS.
requirements.object: the borrowers at the time of obtaining explicit consent from the borrower for availing benefits under a proposed DRS

requirements.full_sentence:
Any waiver of accrued but unrealised interest and/ or sacrifice of principal
undertaken by a NBFC in the borrower accounts of beneficiaries of the DRS,
either as part of the implementation of the scheme or subsequent to its
implementation, shall be treated as a compromise settlement and shall attract the
prudential treatment contained in paragraphs 86 to 100.
requirements.object: compromise settlement and shall attract the prudential treatment contained in paragraphs 86 to 100

conditions:
If the funds received by a NBFC as part of the DRS covers the entire outstanding
dues of the borrower
requirements.full_sentence:
If the funds received by a NBFC as part of the DRS covers the entire outstanding
dues of the borrower, including principal and interest accrued till the date of
<image>
receipt of funds by the NBFC, the same shall lead to extinguishment of borrower's
debt obligations.
requirements.object: borrower's debt obligations

conditions:
where the funds received by a NBFC as part of the scheme are not
adequate to cover the entire outstanding dues of the borrower
requirements.full_sentence:
In cases where the funds received by a NBFC as part of the scheme are not
adequate to cover the entire outstanding dues of the borrower, leading to residual
exposure (principal and / or accrued interest), the asset classification of the
residual exposure shall be evaluated as per the terms and conditions of the
original loan contract.
requirements.object: the terms and conditions of the original loan contract

requirements.full_sentence:
Provided that any changes / modifications to the terms and conditions of the
original loan contract in such cases shall be evaluated against the test of
restructuring as defined in these Directions and shall attract the prudential
treatment therein.
requirements.object: test of restructuring as defined in these Directions and shall attract the prudential treatment therein

requirements.full_sentence:
Any fresh credit exposure to such borrowers shall be as per the commercial
discretion of the NBFC under relevant internal policy, subject to extant applicable
regulations.
requirements.object: commercial discretion of the NBFC under relevant internal policy

requirements.full_sentence:
A NBFC's reporting in respect of the borrowers under the scheme to the credit
information companies shall be guided by the extant guidelines in this regard.
requirements.object: extant guidelines in this regard

requirements.full_sentence:
There shall not be creation of any receivable against the Government on account
of the DRS and the exposure shall continue to be on the borrower till receipt of
funds by the NBFC.
requirements.object: receivable against the Government on account of the DRS and the exposure shall continue to be on the borrower till receipt of funds

requirements.full_sentence:
Till receipt of funds, a NBFC shall continue to apply the prudential norms including
prudential norms on income recognition, asset classification and provisioning,
and wherever the accounts are non-performing, the NBFC may pursue recovery
measures as per their Board approved policy against such borrowers.
requirements.object: prudential norms including prudential norms on income recognition, asset classification and provisioning, and wherever the accou

requirements.full_sentence:
The instructions contained in paragraphs 123 to 134 shall apply in respect of
DRS' notified on or after December 31, 2024 and shall be without prejudice to the
instructions on resolution of stressed assets contained in these Directions.
requirements.object: DRS' notified on or after December 31, 2024 and shall be without prejudice to the instructions on resolution of stressed assets co

requirements.full_sentence:
In respect of relief measures announced prior to December 31, 2024, any dues
pending receipt from Government, for more than 90 days shall attract specific
provision of 100%.

A NBFC shall take necessary action and actively follow up with the respective
Governments for settlement of dues referred to in paragraph 137.
requirements.object: actively follow up with the respective Governments for settlement of dues referred to in paragraph 137

requirements.full_sentence:
The paragraphs 139 to 141 shall be applicable to cases where an act of
restructuring creates new securities issued by the borrower which would be held
by a NBFC in lieu of a portion of the pre-restructured exposure.
requirements.object: where an act of restructuring creates new securities issued by the borrower which would be held by a NBFC in lieu of a portion

requirements.full_sentence:
The Funded Interest Term Loan (FITL) / debt / equity instruments created by
conversion of principal / unpaid interest, as the case may be, shall be placed in
the same asset classification category by a NBFC in which the restructured
advance has been classified.
requirements.object: same asset classification category by a NBFC in which the restructured advance has been classified

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisioning applicable to instruments acquired as part of restructuring shall
be the higher of:

conditions:
in case of FITL or sale / redemption proceeds of the debt /
equity instruments
requirements.full_sentence:
Only on repayment in case of FITL or sale / redemption proceeds of the debt /
equity instruments, the amount received shall be recognised in the Profit and
Loss Account, while simultaneously reducing the balance in the 'Sundry Liabilities
Account (Interest Capitalisation)'.
requirements.object: Profit and Loss Account, while simultaneously reducing the balance in the 'Sundry Liabilities Account (Interest Capitalisation)'

requirements.full_sentence: Valuation and provisioning norms shall be as per paragraphs 216 to 219.

The depreciation, if any, on valuation shall be charged to the Sundry Liabilities
(Interest Capitalisation) Account.
requirements.object: Sundry Liabilities (Interest Capitalisation) Account

requirements.full_sentence:
To mitigate the burden of debt servicing brought about by trade disruptions
caused by global headwinds and to ensure the continuity of viable businesses, a
NBFC may extend relief measures to eligible borrowers, as specified under
Reserve Bank of India (Trade Relief Measures) Directions, 2025 dated November
14, 2025.
requirements.object: eligible borrowers, as specified under Reserve Bank of India (Trade Relief Measures) Directions, 2025 dated November 14, 202

requirements.full_sentence:
The
directions, instructions and guidelines already repealed vide any of the directions,
instructions, and guidelines listed in the above notification shall continue to
remain repealed.

Notwithstanding such repeal, any action taken or purported to have been taken,
or initiated under the repealed Directions, instructions, or guidelines shall
continue to be governed by the provisions thereof.
requirements.object: by the provisions thereof

requirements.full_sentence:
All approvals or
acknowledgments granted under these repealed lists shall be deemed as
governed by these Directions.
requirements.object: by these Directions
affect: (1) any right, obligation or liability acquired, accrued, or incurred thereunder

conditions: if those directions
requirements.full_sentence:
and any such investigation, legal proceedings or remedy may
be instituted, continued, or enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or
punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or
guidelines had not been repealed.
requirements.object: enforced and any such penalty, forfeiture or punishment may be imposed as if those directions, instructions, or guideline

requirements.full_sentence:
The provisions of these Directions shall be in addition to, and not in derogation of
the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time
being in force.
requirements.object: and not in derogation of the provisions of any other laws, rules, regulations, or directions, for the time being in force

conditions: if it considers necessary
requirements.full_sentence:
For the purpose of giving effect to the provisions of these Directions or in order
to remove any difficulties in the application or interpretation of the provisions of
these Directions, the Reserve Bank may, if it considers necessary, issue
necessary clarifications in respect of any matter covered herein and the
interpretation of any provision of these Directions given by the Reserve Bank shall
be final and binding.

For the purpose of the Model Operating Procedure (MOP), Debt Relief Schemes
(DRS) refer to Schemes notified by the State Governments that entail funding by the
fiscal authorities to cover debt obligations of a targeted segment of borrowers that the
regulated entities are required to sacrifice / waive.

Announcement / notification of any such DRS should include the specific stress or
distress situation necessitating announcement of such support.
requirements.object: or distress situation necessitating announcement of such support

requirements.full_sentence:
Given the broader
implications of such DRS for the credit culture, while broad based relief measures can
be addressed through pure fiscal support in the form of Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT),
DRS should be considered only as a measure of last resort when other measures to
alleviate financial stress have failed.
requirements.object: a measure of last resort when other measures to alleviate financial stress have failed

requirements.full_sentence:
Before announcing any DRS, Governments may engage with the State Level
Bankers' Committee (SLBC)/ District level Consultative Committee (DCC) to evolve a
coordinated action plan for conceptualisation, design, and implementation of the DRS.
requirements.object: Level Bankers' Committee (SLBC)/ District level Consultative Committee (DCC) to evolve a coordinated action plan for conceptuali

requirements.full_sentence:
The schemes should, cover critical aspects of the scheme like identification of
borrowers, impact assessment, implementation timelines, resolution of issues
concerning settlement of dues by Government to the lending institutions, etc.
requirements.object: of the scheme like identification of borrowers, impact assessment, implementation timelines, resolution of issues concerning

requirements.full_sentence:
The design features should ensure that the DRS do not impact the financial stability
aspects of the region / State or create moral hazards in the borrower segments.
requirements.object: do not impact the financial stability aspects of the region / State or create moral hazards in the borrower segments

requirements.full_sentence: should also be taken into account.
requirements.object: account

requirements.full_sentence:
Detailed budgetary provisions / funding may be provided upfront towards any
proposed DRS to fully cover the required settlement amounts.
requirements.object: any proposed DRS to fully cover the required settlement amounts

conditions:
Where regulated entities
have dues from the Government
requirements.full_sentence:
Where regulated entities
have dues from the Government, pertaining to earlier DRS schemes, new schemes
should be announced only on a fully pre-funded basis.
requirements.object: a fully pre-funded basis

requirements.full_sentence:
The DRS should be targeted only at the impacted borrowers and should not contain
any restrictive covenant against timely repayments.
requirements.object: the impacted borrowers and should not contain any restrictive covenant against timely repayments

requirements.full_sentence:
Further, it should specify the
criteria for determining eligible borrowers on an objective basis, detailed timeline of
critical / material events, including cut-off dates for filing/ submission,
acknowledgement, approval and settlement of claims along with compensation
clauses for delays in settling the funds, on part of the Government.
requirements.object: determining eligible borrowers on an objective basis, detailed timeline of critical / material events, including cut-off date

requirements.full_sentence:
The DRS should cover the entire outstanding dues of the borrowers being covered,
including principal and accumulated interest till the date of receipt of funds by the
regulated entities from the Government.
requirements.object: dues of the borrowers being covered, including principal and accumulated interest till the date of receipt of funds by th

requirements.full_sentence:
The DRS should not require the creation of a receivable in the books of the regulated
entity against the Government.
requirements.object: a receivable in the books of the regulated entity against the Government

requirements.full_sentence:
The exposure of regulated entities to the borrower shall
continue and shall be reduced to the extent of funds received from the Government.
requirements.object: reduced to the extent of funds received from the Government

requirements.full_sentence:
The entire implementation of the Scheme and settlement of claims by the
Governments to the regulated entities, should generally be completed within 45 to 60
days.
requirements.object: 45 to 60 days

requirements.full_sentence:
The DRS should not contain any provision contrary to any regulatory instruction
issued by RBI / NABARD.
requirements.object: to any regulatory instruction issued by RBI / NABARD

requirements.full_sentence:
The design of the DRS should not contain any provision that casts any obligations
on the regulated entities, directly or indirectly, to:
a.
requirements.object: casts any obligations on the regulated entities, directly or indirectly, to: a

conditions:
if the regulated entities agree to any of the above at the time of design of
DRS or subsequently
requirements.full_sentence:
However, if the regulated entities agree to any of the above at the time of design of
DRS or subsequently, as per their Board-approved policies, it shall be subject to the
applicable prudential guidelines.
requirements.object: applicable prudential guidelines
